Package org.jlab.epsci.ersap.engine

Interface Engine

All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractEventReaderService, AbstractEventWriterService, AbstractService, AbstractStreamSourceService, DataManager, E1, E2, E3, E4, Empty, Factorial
public interface Engine
Service engine interface.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    EngineData
    configure(EngineData input)
    Configures the engine with the given input data.
    void
    destroy()
    Destroy this engine, closing and cleaning up any opened resources.
    EngineData
    execute(EngineData input)
    Executes the engine with the given input data.
    EngineData
    executeGroup(Set<EngineData> inputs)
    Executes the engine with the given set of input data.
    String
    getAuthor()
    Gets the author(s) of the engine.
    String
    getDescription()
    Gets a description of the engine.
    Set<EngineDataType>
    getInputDataTypes()
    Gets the set of input data types supported by the engine.
    Set<EngineDataType>
    getOutputDataTypes()
    Gets the set of output data types supported by the engine.
    Set<String>
    getStates()
    Gets the set of possible states for the engine results.
    String
    getVersion()
    Gets the version of the engine.
    void
    reset()
    Resets this engine to its initial setup.

  • Method Details

    • configure

      EngineData configure(EngineData input)
      Configures the engine with the given input data.

      This method can be executed concurrently in several threads.

      Parameters:
      input - the data to configure the engine with
      Returns:
      an (optional) result and/or status of the configuration request, it could be null or without output data (but not recommended)

    • execute

      EngineData execute(EngineData input)
      Executes the engine with the given input data.

      This method can be executed concurrently in several threads.

      Parameters:
      input - the data to execute the engine with
      Returns:
      the result and/or status of the execution request, it cannot be null or without data (unless it is an error status), and it should not be the same input data reference

    • executeGroup

      EngineData executeGroup(Set<EngineData> inputs)
      Executes the engine with the given set of input data.

      This method can be executed concurrently in several threads.

      Parameters:
      inputs - the data set to execute the engine with
      Returns:
      the result and/or status of the execution request, it cannot be null or without data (unless it is an error status), and it should not be the same as one of the input data references

    • getInputDataTypes

      Set<EngineDataType> getInputDataTypes()
      Gets the set of input data types supported by the engine.
      Returns:
      the allowed input data types, it cannot be null nor empty

    • getOutputDataTypes

      Set<EngineDataType> getOutputDataTypes()
      Gets the set of output data types supported by the engine.
      Returns:
      the allowed output data types, it cannot be null nor empty

    • getStates

      Set<String> getStates()
      Gets the set of possible states for the engine results. States should be set as the result of processing a request and stored in the result data. Orchestrators and clients of the engine can use the states as part of an application composition to provide advanced routing of the execution outputs.
      Returns:
      a set with the possible states for the engine results, it can be null or empty if the engine doesn't uses states

    • getDescription

      String getDescription()
      Gets a description of the engine.

      A good description should provide an initial summary sentence about the engine, a section with complete details of what the engine does, how to configure the service and the supported data types, all the requests that the engine can handle, with the expected input types and values and the resulting output types and values, the returned errors for failed requests, and a changelog for the current version.

      Returns:
      a string with the description

    • getVersion

      String getVersion()
      Gets the version of the engine. Examples are: a semver release number, a git describe output, an alpha or beta testing release, etc.
      Returns:
      a version string

    • getAuthor

      String getAuthor()
      Gets the author(s) of the engine. Authors are responsible of developing and maintaining a service engine.

      This information should provide at least a name and a contact email.

      Returns:
      an string listing the author(s) of the engine.

    • reset

      void reset()
      Resets this engine to its initial setup. It should at least revert any changes done by configuration requests and side-effects of execute requests.

    • destroy

      void destroy()
      Destroy this engine, closing and cleaning up any opened resources.
